sections.heading: What is RemoteOps?
sections.body: RemoteOps is an open-source remote server management dashboard. It lets you connect to your servers via SSH directly from the browser, manage team access through groups, and keep all your connections organised in one place.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body: You need a RemoteOps account (free), and your target server must accept SSH connections on port 22 or a custom port. No client-side SSH software is required.

sections.heading: 1. Create an account
sections.body: Head to /signup and fill in your username, email and password. You will receive a verification email. Click the link to activate your account.
sections.code: # No CLI needed. Everything lives in the browser.

sections.heading: 2. Add your first connection
sections.body: From the Dashboard click "Add Connection". Enter a display name, the server's IP address or hostname, the SSH port, and the username you use to log in.

sections.heading: 3. Launch the terminal
sections.body: Click the connection ID in the dashboard table. RemoteOps opens a live SSH session in your browser powered by xterm.js and a WebSocket tunnel. No local terminal needed.

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: RemoteOps proxies your SSH session through a Node.js WebSocket server using the ssh2 library. Your browser connects to the WebSocket endpoint and the server performs the actual SSH handshake on your behalf.

sections.heading: Session security
sections.body: Sessions are authenticated via your RemoteOps cookie. The server verifies your session before opening any SSH connection. Connections are scoped to your user or your groups, so there is no cross-user access.

sections.heading: Reconnecting
sections.body: If the WebSocket drops due to a network blip or server restart, refresh the page and re-open the connection from the dashboard. Session state on the remote server is preserved as long as your shell session is still alive.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Groups let you share server connections with teammates without handing out credentials. Create a group, add connections to it, and invite members by email.

sections.heading: Creating a group
sections.body: Go to Dashboard, then Manage Groups, then Create Group. Give the group a name and you become the admin automatically.

sections.heading: Inviting members
sections.body: From the group detail page, enter a team member's registered RemoteOps email and click Invite. They will see the group and its connections in their dashboard right away.

sections.heading: Admin controls
sections.body: As the group admin you can remove members, delete the group, and add or remove connections. Regular members can view and open connections but cannot modify the group.

sections.heading: Permissions summary
sections.code:
Admin   create, read, update, delete group
        add/remove members
        add/remove connections

Member  read group
        open connections (SSH)

sections.heading: Session cookies
sections.body: RemoteOps uses HttpOnly session cookies, not localStorage .

sections.heading: Email verification
sections.body: After signup you receive a verification link. Unverified accounts cannot open SSH sessions. The link expires after some time and you can request a new one from the login page.

sections.heading: Password reset
sections.body: Visit /forgot-password, enter your email, and you will receive a time-limited reset link.

sections.heading: Logout
sections.body: Clicking Logout issues a server-side cookie deletion. The session is invalidated on the backend before the cookie is cleared in the browser.

sections.heading: User
sections.code:
POST   /user/signup                    register a new user
POST   /user/login                     login and receive session cookie
GET    /user/logout                    invalidate session
GET    /user/me                        get current user info
GET    /user/verify/:token             verify email
POST   /user/forgot-password           request password reset
PATCH  /user/reset-password/:token     reset password

sections.heading: Connections
sections.code:
POST   /connection/add                              add a connection
GET    /connection/user-connections/personal         list personal connections
GET    /connection/:id                              get connection by ID
PATCH  /connection/edit/:id                         update connection
DELETE /connection/delete/:id                       delete connection

sections.heading: Groups
sections.code:
POST   /group/create               create a group
GET    /group/user-groups            list groups for current user
GET    /group/:id                   get group detail
POST   /group/:id/invite            invite a member
DELETE /group/:id/member/:uid       remove a member
DELETE /group/:id                   delete group (admin only)
